policy._comment: Keystone governance policy as code. Canonical sha256 of this file is pinned into every decision in the audit ledger, so an auditor can reconstruct the exact policy in force at any historical approval. Tiers map the engine-computed blast radius (distinct affected files) to a required approver count, a minimum review window, and an ALLOW / HOLD / BLOCK action. Deterministic: same graph snapshot + same policy => same tier. No model.

agents._comment: Keystone agent registry as code. Maps an autonomous coding agent's author id to a scope manifest enforced deterministically (fnmatch path globs + max blast radius). A change an agent opens outside this scope is a hard SCOPE-VIOLATION. Matching is literal; there is no ML and no fuzzy matching. A registry miss is reported as AGENT_UNREGISTERED (detected, not proven). Editing this file is itself a governed change.
